mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly auto sales indicates weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields an insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a panel regression of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: The industrial production index exceeding 105 signals supply-side overheating and capacity constraints, prompting short-term profit-taking and negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where INDPRO exceeds 105.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with stagnant federal funds rates signals delayed monetary tightening, causing negative equity returns as markets price in future rate hikes.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 65 percent of instances where HOUST falls below 900.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment confirms a broad economic contraction, triggering immediate negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 10-year sample.
